questions.question: What is climate?
questions.answer: Climate refers to the sum total of weather conditions and variations over a large area for a long period of time.

questions.question: What is the difference between weather and climate?
questions.answer: Weather refers to the condition of the atmosphere at a particular time, while climate refers to the average weather conditions over a long period.

questions.question: Name the main elements of weather and climate.
questions.answer: Temperature, atmospheric pressure, wind, humidity and precipitation.

questions.question: What is meant by monsoon?
questions.answer: Monsoon refers to the seasonal reversal of wind direction during a year.

questions.question: Why is the climate of India called a monsoon type?
questions.answer: Because the seasonal reversal of winds brings rainfall to most parts of the country.

questions.question: What are climatic controls?
questions.answer: Climatic controls are factors that influence the climate of a place such as latitude, altitude and distance from the sea.

questions.question: Name any two climatic controls.
questions.answer: Latitude and altitude.

questions.question: Why are hill stations cooler than plains?
questions.answer: Because temperature decreases with increase in altitude.

questions.question: What role do the Himalayas play in India's climate?
questions.answer: The Himalayas prevent cold winds from Central Asia from entering India.

questions.question: What is the cold weather season in India?
questions.answer: It is the winter season that occurs from mid-November to February.

questions.question: Which months are the coldest in northern India?
questions.answer: December and January are the coldest months.

questions.question: What are western disturbances?
questions.answer: Western disturbances are cyclonic storms originating in the Mediterranean region that bring winter rainfall to northern India.

questions.question: What is the hot weather season?
questions.answer: The hot weather season occurs from March to May and is characterized by high temperatures.

questions.question: What are 'loo' winds?
questions.answer: Loo are strong, hot and dry winds that blow in northern India during summer.

questions.question: What are mango showers?
questions.answer: Mango showers are pre-monsoon rains that help ripen mangoes.

questions.question: When does the advancing monsoon begin?
questions.answer: It begins in early June when southwest monsoon winds reach India.

questions.question: Name the two branches of the southwest monsoon.
questions.answer: Arabian Sea branch and Bay of Bengal branch.

questions.question: What is retreating monsoon?
questions.answer: It is the period when the southwest monsoon winds withdraw from India during October and November.

questions.question: Why does Tamil Nadu receive rainfall in winter?
questions.answer: Because of the retreating monsoon winds.

questions.question: Which place receives the highest rainfall in the world?
questions.answer: Mawsynram in Meghalaya receives the highest rainfall.

questions.question: Which region receives the least rainfall in India?
questions.answer: Western Rajasthan receives very little rainfall.

questions.question: Why does rainfall decrease from east to west in the northern plains?
questions.answer: Because the monsoon winds lose moisture as they move westward.

questions.question: What are cyclones?
questions.answer: Cyclones are powerful storms with strong winds and heavy rainfall.

questions.question: What is meant by break in monsoon?
questions.answer: Break in monsoon refers to short periods when monsoon rainfall stops temporarily.

questions.question: Why is monsoon important for India?
questions.answer: Monsoon provides water for agriculture and supports the livelihoods of millions of people.
